steps.title: Identify the passage's reframing signal
steps.content: The passage rejects the gender-focused reading as incomplete because it "leaves the presence of Valdez's male collaborator Sandoval unexplained." The author then signals a pivot with "We should instead consider" — pointing toward the art establishment's institutional constraints on which types of Chicano artists it recognizes, not social constraints on women.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'while Valdez's presence in Instant Mural represents the social constraints placed on women at the time, Sandoval's presence represents Chicano muralists' frustration at their lack of recognition by the art establishment': Splits the two figures into different readings but still ties Valdez to gender constraints — the passage rejects gender-constraint framing for the work as a whole. ✗
  • Choice B — 'the main subject of Instant Mural is female Chicano artists experience of being doubly constrained by gender-role expectations and the marginalization of certain types of art': Keeps the gender focus as primary — exactly what the passage's "We should instead consider" pivot overrules. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Instant Mural is best understood not as a critique of the social constraints placed on women but rather as a critique of sociohistorical muralists' depictions of Chicano culture': Pivots away from gender but lands on muralists' depictions, which is not the institutional-recognition argument the passage makes. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Instant Mural is a reflection on the constraining aesthetic expectations placed on Chicano artists in general rather than on the social constraints placed on women specifically': Precisely captures the "instead consider" pivot — institutional aesthetic constraints on all Chicano artists, not gendered constraints on women. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
